# Konde Design Framework (KDF) Playground - Full Documentation

## About KDF Playground
The KDF Playground is an interactive, web-based tool designed to help developers and AI agents experiment with the Konde Design Framework (KDF). It allows users to write raw JSON configuration (design tokens) and instantly preview how those tokens are compiled and rendered into beautiful, Tailwind CSS-powered UI components.

## What is KDF?
The Konde Design Framework is a unified design system that enables rapid, consistent, and beautiful UI development across the Konde ecosystem. It is built from the ground up to be AI-native, meaning both human developers and autonomous AI agents can effortlessly construct UIs by writing strictly-typed JSON schemas rather than dealing with massive, messy HTML/CSS directly.

## JSON Token Structure
A typical KDF component is defined using a JSON object with the following schema:
- `"$"`: The element type or component name (e.g., "button", "card").
- `"className"`: The base Tailwind utility classes for the component.
- `"variants"`: A dictionary of style variations (e.g., "default", "destructive", "outline").
- `"defaultVariants"`: The fallback variants to apply if none are explicitly requested.

### Example: KDF Button
```json
{
  "$": "button",
  "className": "inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-lg px-8 py-4 text-sm font-semibold tracking-wide text-white shadow-lg transition-all",
  "variants": {
    "default": "bg-green-600 hover:bg-green-700",
    "destructive": "bg-red-500 hover:bg-red-600"
  },
  "defaultVariants": {
    "variant": "default"
  }
}
```

## Developer Resources & Technical Documentation
For autonomous agents and developers, the full source code and technical documentation are available in our open-source repository:
- **Main Repository**: [KondeIO/konde-io](https://github.com/KondeIO/konde-io)
- **README**: [konde-io/README.md](https://github.com/KondeIO/konde-io/blob/main/README.md)
- **Technical Docs**: [konde-io/docs/](https://github.com/KondeIO/konde-io/tree/main/docs)
